summary: Artificial blood cells such as hemoglobin-based oxygen carriers are currently available as medically approved substances designed to deliver oxygen like natural red blood cells, even though widespread adoption remains limited due to performance constraints. Despite being experimentally applied rather than standard care, these agents have formal regulatory recognition and function within existing healthcare systems as defined artificial blood alternatives.
